findings.invariant: Ontology and boundary ownership must be explicit in docs.
findings.observed: Architecture docs explicitly assign ownership to PlatformManifest, CxRP, RxP, OperationsCenter, ExecutorRuntime, PlatformDeployment, managed private project, and Custodian.
findings.expected: No ambiguous or overlapping ownership language.
findings.recommendation: Keep docs and ownership tests in sync when roles evolve.

findings.observed: CxRP still defines TaskProposal, LaneDecision, ExecutionRequest, ExecutionResult, RuntimeBinding, and CapabilitySet in its own repo.
findings.expected: Execution/routing contracts live in CxRP, not PlatformManifest.
findings.recommendation: Continue referencing these from PlatformManifest rather than duplicating them.

findings.observed: RxP still defines RuntimeInvocation and RuntimeResult in its own repo.
findings.expected: Runtime invocation contracts live in RxP, not PlatformManifest.
findings.recommendation: Keep invocation and transport semantics owned in RxP.

findings.invariant: Private manifest kind must exist in model and schema surface.
findings.observed: ManifestKind.PRIVATE, private_manifest.schema.json, and private-manifest composition support are implemented.
findings.expected: A first-class private-manifest shape distinct from project scope.
findings.recommendation: Keep new private-manifest fixtures and docs aligned with the implementation.

findings.invariant: Ontology relationships must support the requested relationship vocabulary.
findings.observed: OntologyRelationshipKind, OntologyRelationship, relationship schemas, and RepoGraph relationship queries are implemented.
findings.expected: Query-capable ontology relationship kinds must be model-visible and schema-visible.
findings.recommendation: Preserve relationship query coverage as new kinds are added.

findings.invariant: Entity and relationship models should explicitly represent projection metadata.
findings.observed: Projection metadata fields exist on RepoNode and OntologyRelationship and are used by to_public_manifest_dict.
findings.expected: Explicit projection metadata fields and fail-closed semantics.
findings.recommendation: Keep projection behavior modeled explicitly rather than migrating it back into ad hoc metadata.

findings.invariant: Public manifests must be safe projections of private or effective input.
findings.observed: Projection tests prove stripping of private nodes, private-only relationships, local fields, and non-public projection behavior.
findings.recommendation: Keep these tests as a release gate.

findings.invariant: Public publication should not bypass projection validation accidentally.
findings.observed: project-public always validates, and unsafe generation moved to a separate project-public-unsafe command with an explicit warning.
findings.expected: No silent validation bypass on the normal publication path.
findings.recommendation: Keep release and publication workflows on project-public only.

findings.invariant: PlatformManifest should own PMV-specific policy logic; Custodian should own runtime and reporting interfaces.
findings.observed: PlatformManifest still exports the PMV detector contributor, and Custodian still loads it through a generic contributor target.
findings.expected: Plugin/native detector ownership split with stable PMV rule IDs.
findings.recommendation: Keep PMV semantics versioned in PlatformManifest.

findings.invariant: OperationsCenter must consume PlatformManifest without owning ontology.
findings.observed: OC repo_graph_factory now composes platform -> private -> (project xor work_scope) -> local and remains a metadata consumer only.
findings.expected: OC remains a topology and visibility consumer and orchestration owner only.
findings.recommendation: Keep manifest parsing centralized in repo_graph_factory and keep private-manifest discovery tested.

findings.invariant: Semantic duplication across PlatformManifest, CxRP, RxP, and OperationsCenter should remain bounded.
findings.observed: OperationsCenter now uses explicit OC-native names for internal planning, routing, and execution-boundary models and maps them explicitly to canonical CxRP contracts at repo boundaries.
findings.expected: Internal OC models remain clearly non-canonical and boundary mapping stays explicit and tested.
findings.recommendation: Keep contract ownership tests and mapper coverage in place as the internal OC model layer evolves.
